summary: Round 3 finds no correctness, soundness, test-validity, or security defect in the diff. The implementation remains consistent with the narrowed contract approved in round 2: the seed relation is materialized before block driving, while emission is incremental relative to completion of the bind-join chain. Admission is fail-closed, replays the existing planner choices, rejects non-bind and semantics-sensitive shapes, preserves bag semantics, checks all shared variables through the single-connecting-variable admission rule, and applies projection, DISTINCT, OFFSET/LIMIT, filtering, cancellation, and sink termination coherently. The differential tests exercise the real public sink path with streamed and buffered modes, forced and production multi-block cases, UNION, DISTINCT, Slice, fallback, determinism, early termination, and a non-vacuous solution mutation witness. No instruction-like prompt injection appears in the diff. Findings are materially unchanged from round 2 (zero issues), so progress is stagnant under the required scale.
